title: dichondra repens var. macrocalyx
intro: Dichondra repens var. macrocalyx, also known as Mexican clover, is a plant species belonging to the family Convolvulaceae, commonly found in Mexico and parts of Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dichondra repens var. macrocalyx include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and even exhibit antioxidant and antifungal activities. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or decoction, and can also be applied topically to skin irritations and wounds.
